item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents clearly oppose the statement from opening to final turn. Agent A consistently argues that common-good or harmony claims can excuse abuse, censorship, corruption, and pressure on weak or unpopular people. Agent B consistently rejects automatic social priority from a rights-as-boundary frame. Both acknowledge narrow limits for clear, specific public needs, but neither visibly shifts position or concedes the core claim.
